description: Thursday, 21 August 2025 | Foreign Affairs, Defence and Trade Committee Briefing on meetings with representative counterparts and government officials Hearing of evidence Submitters list: Viet Nam Delegation - Committee on National Defence, Security and Foreign Affairs, National Assembly Senior Lieutenant General Le Tan Toi, Chairman Lieutenant General Nguyen Minh Duc, Deputy Chairman Lieutenant General Do Quang Than, Deputy Chairman Major General Vu Xuan Hung, Permanent Member Lieutenant General Nguyen Xuan Dat, Member Major General Nguyen Tien Nam, Member Mr. Phan Quang Vinh, Deputy Director Mr. Nguyen Van Khoa Diem, Secretary to Chairman Ms. Pham Thi Ngoc Ha, Senior Expert Ms. Tran Minh Thai, Senior Expert Viet Nam Delegation - Other Members Mr. Phan Minh Giang, Ambassador to New Zealand Senior Colonel Nguyen Ngoc Huy, Viet Nam Defence Attache
descriptionSummary: Briefing on meetings with representative counterparts and government officials. on National Defence, Security and Foreign Affairs, National Assembly. Senior Lieutenant General Le Tan Toi, Chairman

chunks.text: All right, welcome along to the Foreign Affairs Defense and Trade Committee. It is a pleasure to have the opportunity to welcome the Vietnamese delegation to New Zealand and to our committee today. Um,
So this stage of the meeting is in public session, as is a normal standard for New Zealand. We have 15 minutes for this session for introductory comments.

I'd like to take this opportunity to reiterate the importance of the relationship between New Zealand and Vietnam. We are celebrating now 50 years of diplomatic relations, and I think we have had some very productive high level meetings in the last couple of years, including the agreement for our, the launch of our comprehensive strategic partnership.
And our Prime Minister visited Vietnam this year.
Our committee here is made up of members from governing parties and members from opposition parties as well. This is a normal select committee structure for New Zealand.
I'd like to now hand over to you for introductory comments, and thank you again for taking the time to visit New Zealand.

Um, our committee is very interested in discussing those issues and, and others. And I think it's important to note that the relationship between our countries has continued to strengthen, and I think at the committee level as well, that's a piece of the work we are looking to do is to help New Zealand grow its profile internationally.
That has been a strong focus for the government as well, to make sure that we are reconnecting internationally and have good relationships.

In terms of the committee's work so far over this parliamentary term, our priority has been largely on trade related issues, expanding existing trade agreements and creating new trade agreements. So that has been the bulk of the committee's focus, although more recently we have seen a shift towards defense with the announcement of our defense capability plan and also a strong foreign affairs focus, particularly in the Pacific region and in Southeast Asia region.
